top_weaknesses.issue: Multiple paragraphs >80 words (P6 ~101, P8 ~93, P9 ~91, P12 ~82) — wall of text on mobile
top_weaknesses.fix: Split each at the natural transition; aim for <80 words per paragraph

top_weaknesses.issue: Stat lede over-leveraged on a low-trust source (61% / $155M); also unlinked name-drops (WHU, Foundation Capital) reading as citation-scented synthesis
top_weaknesses.fix: Cite primary source or downgrade to directional claim; link or remove the WHU and Foundation Capital references

top_weaknesses.issue: No concrete example anchoring the thesis; reads as investor-memo trend essay (NFX/WHU/FC frameworks stacked) without one named distribution-vs-product battle
top_weaknesses.fix: Insert one specific case: a vertical AI startup that won via EHR/EMR partnership, or a generic agent that lost despite better benchmarks

top_weaknesses.issue: Stops at diagnosis; no 'Monday morning' tactical payoff
top_weaknesses.fix: Add an H2 like 'How to Build Distribution as a Product' with 3-5 levers (partnerships, compliance, procurement, implementation, anchor customer)

consensus_summary: Strong spine and a genuinely shareable closing line ('it does not yet have a startup. It has a technical artifact.'), but the post is over-leveraged on shaky stats and stays at framework-stacking altitude. Tighten paragraphs for mobile, source or remove the numbers, and add one concrete embed-vs-cold-email example.
